expectations.evidence: Creates a DashboardLayout React component with sidebar, header, and main content area. Component is defined as an arrow function with TypeScript props interface and JSX layout structure.

Extensively uses Tailwind CSS utility classes throughout: flex, h-screen, bg-bg-primary, transition-transform, grid layout classes, responsive prefixes (md:, lg:, sm:), spacing (px-4, py-2.5, gap-3), etc.

Component contains no business logic. All callbacks (onNavItemClick, onNotificationClick, onProfileClick, onSearchChange) are placeholder props passed in. Only local state is sidebar open/close toggle which is pure UI state. Explicitly states 'No business logic | Per layout-first principle: visual only'.

eval_feedback.overall: The with_skill run for eval-19 produced a thorough, well-structured dashboard layout component that closely follows the ui-publish skill workflow. It references design system docs, applies Tailwind CSS with semantic tokens, uses proper naming conventions (handle/on prefixes), and keeps the component logic-free with placeholder callback props.
